title: Code LLMs
concept: How code-specialized models are trained and how their attention layers handle syntax structure compared to prose.

options.text: A model that generates and understands coding syntax

exercises.explain: A code-specialized model is designed to understand and generate coding syntax, similar to how language models understand prose.

cards.title: What Are Code LLMs?
cards.body: Code LLMs, or Language Learning Models, are tools specifically trained to understand and write code. They help programmers by suggesting code snippets and explaining syntax.

cards.title: Training Code Models
cards.body: Code LLMs are trained on vast amounts of code from various programming languages, allowing them to learn the syntax, rules, and common patterns in coding.

cards.title: Building with Blocks
cards.body: Imagine learning to build a structure with blocks. Code LLMs are like builders learning the rules of how blocks fit together to create a stable structure. Code must follow specific rules, just like building does.

cards.title: Example of Code Generation
cards.body: If a user types 'def function_name:', a code LLM might generate the rest of a function based on learned structure, similar to finishing a sentence.

cards.title: Myth: Code Models Know Everything
cards.body: It's a common myth that code LLMs know all programming languages perfectly. In reality, their knowledge depends on the data they were trained on.

cards.title: Syntax vs. Prose
cards.body: Code has strict rules (syntax) while prose is more flexible and expressive.

left.text: Flexible, expressive, and open to interpretation.

right.text: Strict, follows defined rules and structures.

exercises.prompt: What do code-specialized models focus on when analyzing code?

options.text: The feelings expressed in the code
The syntax and structure of the code
The length of the code in words

exercises.explain: Code-specialized models pay close attention to the syntax and structure to ensure the code is valid and functions as intended.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Code LLMs are trained only on stories and poems.
exercises.explain: It's false; Code LLMs are not trained on stories and poems but rather on code from programming languages.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: Code models learn the ______ and rules of programming languages.
exercises.sentence: Code models learn the syntax and rules of programming languages.
exercises.explain: The word 'syntax' reflects the focus of code models on the strict structure of code.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following statements is true about code LLMs?

options.text: They can write music based on emotional tone.
They focus on particular syntax and structure in coding.
They are only for understanding prose.

exercises.explain: Code LLMs primarily focus on syntax and structure, which is crucial for generating functional and valid code.

exercises.prompt: How do attention layers in code LLMs differ from those in prose models?

options.text: They focus more on visual layout than textual meaning.
They pay attention to specific syntax and structure in the code instead of general context.
They ignore punctuation, which is vital for code.
They analyze sentences like a human reader without focusing on syntax.

exercises.explain: Attention layers in code LLMs concentrate on the precise syntax and structure required in programming languages, unlike prose models that consider broader context and expressions.
